issue: dingo-gw/dingo#327 — pass network metadata (not the 5GB network) into generation/plotting
caveat: Caveats: (1) All 11 runs used the same model and identical task prompt; only the AGENTS.md / agent-config content differed. (2) cedric is a full Claude Code config (skills, subagent, hooks, settings); since a spawned sub-agent cannot auto-fire hooks/skills, its config content was injected into the prompt — so it measures cedric's guidance, not live automation. (3) The objective test checks whether the model metadata is recoverable from the completed-config bundle (inline JSON/base64 or a referenced pickle/torch sidecar all count); baseline HEAD scores 0.26 and fails. (4) Quality is a blind rating by an independent reviewer agent per diff; the agent 'summary' shown is each fix's own serialization approach.

runs.name: control
runs.label: 00 · no instructions (control)
runs.approach: base64(JSON) inline in config; noted '#' ini-truncation risk and avoided it via base64
runs.quality_justification: Metadata serialization (base64+JSON) and network exclusion correctly implemented with round-trip robustness and backward-compatible fallback, but lacks JSON-serialization error handling and tests.
runs.summary: base64(JSON) inline in config; noted '#' ini-truncation risk and avoided it via base64

runs.name: minimal
runs.label: 01 · minimal (~11 ln)
runs.approach: pickle sidecar file referenced by --model-metadata path
runs.quality_justification: Correct metadata extraction/consumption via pickle sidecar, but breaks backward compatibility by removing model transfer with no fallback for old configs lacking the metadata file.
runs.summary: pickle sidecar file referenced by --model-metadata path

runs.name: lean
runs.label: 02 · lean (~23 ln)
runs.approach: JSON inline in config; ran black
runs.quality_justification: Sound core JSON design but critical missing test update (EXCLUDED_FROM_INI) causes a test failure that would block merge.
runs.summary: JSON inline in config; ran black

runs.name: commands_first
runs.label: 03 · commands-first (~33 ln)
runs.approach: JSON inline in config; fallback load; black on changed files
runs.quality_justification: Correct JSON-in-config fix with backward-compatible fallback; minor concern is no error handling if metadata is not JSON-serializable.
runs.summary: JSON inline in config; fallback load; black on changed files

runs.name: core_standards
runs.label: 04 · core standards (~41 ln)
runs.approach: JSON inline in config; guard on manual set; black
runs.quality_justification: Comprehensive fix: extracts metadata to JSON, writes to config, consumes in generation, stops network transfer, with solid backward-compatible fallback.
runs.summary: JSON inline in config; guard on manual set; black

runs.name: workflow_discipline
runs.label: 05 · workflow/discipline (~31 ln)
runs.quality_justification: Well-implemented torch.save sidecar carrying metadata through config; removes network transfer and keeps backward compatibility; minor style/robustness refinements.

runs.name: technical_reference
runs.label: 06 · technical reference (~42 ln)
runs.approach: compact JSON inline in config; verified domain rebuild
runs.quality_justification: Well-executed compact-JSON fix; properly integrates with bilby_pipe parser, clean fallback, minimally invasive.
runs.summary: compact JSON inline in config; verified domain rebuild

runs.name: pipe_focused
runs.label: 07 · pipe-focused (~36 ln)
runs.approach: JSON inline in config; guard on manual set
runs.quality_justification: Correctly embeds JSON metadata in the complete config, eliminating the network transfer while keeping backward compatibility; edge-case handling for missing metadata could be more defensive.
runs.summary: JSON inline in config; guard on manual set

runs.name: audit_strategy
runs.label: 08 · audit/strategy (~38 ln)
runs.quality_justification: High-quality fix using str(dict)+ast.literal_eval with robust round-trip, backward-compatible fallback, and correct titled-group placement; not verified in integration.

runs.name: comprehensive
runs.label: 09 · comprehensive (~177 ln)
runs.approach: JSON inline; guard; removed unused import; black
runs.quality_justification: Core fix correct and complete, but no error handling for missing metadata and no backward-compatibility fallback; JSON safety claimed but unverified.
runs.summary: JSON inline; guard; removed unused import; black

runs.name: cedric
runs.label: 10 · cedric (full .claude config)
runs.approach: JSON inline (titled group so it serializes); guard; black per hook
runs.quality_justification: Core fix correct and avoids network transfer, but critically omits updating EXCLUDED_FROM_INI in test_parser_ini_consistency.py, causing a test failure.
runs.summary: JSON inline (titled group so it serializes); guard; black per hook
